close_reason: Added ?namespace= param support with X-Vault-Namespace header in get() and put(), with tests
description:
Vault Enterprise multi-tenancy uses namespaces, which require the X-Vault-Namespace header on every request. This crate hardcodes only X-Vault-Token (lib.rs:197, 301). Without namespace support, this crate is unusable on Vault Enterprise deployments that namespace KV mounts (which is most enterprise deployments).

Bad behavior: Vault Enterprise users get HTTP 403 with confusing error 'permission denied' even with a correct token, because the request lands in the root namespace where the mount does not exist.

Fix: support an optional VAULT_NAMESPACE env var and/or ?namespace= URI param; emit X-Vault-Namespace when set. README should document this.

This is a maintainer-burden finding rather than a bug because the crate works correctly for Vault OSS. Filing as P2 because it gates an entire user population (Vault Enterprise) but is not a regression.
title: Vault Enterprise namespaces unsupported — no X-Vault-Namespace header
